rationale: PR changes the BBDC resolver's behavior when an @-mentioner has no OpenHands account: instead of silently running as the installer, it now refuses and posts a sign-up reply. enterprise/integrations/bitbucket_data_center/bitbucket_dc_manager.py adds `_send_user_not_found_message` (reusing the shared `get_user_not_found_message` copy), introduces a `lookup_failed` flag so transient Keycloak errors `return` early rather than fall back to the installer, and replaces the prior `mentioner_keycloak_id = installer_user_id` fallback with a call to `_send_user_not_found_message(...)` + `return`. The test file rewrites two existing tests to assert `mock_start.assert_not_called()` plus `mock_not_found.assert_awaited_once()` (with arg index 1 == 'kc-installer' and arg index 2 == 'alice'), drops the prior 'falls back to installer' tests, and adds a new test_send_user_not_found_message_replies_as_installer asserting the factory call kwargs, sentinel_view propagation, 'sign up' in body, and '@alice' in body. Test changes encode the new contract; not assertion weakening — the new assertions are stricter and align with the intended behavior change.
